revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 上今井香取神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=江戸川区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[江戸川区]][[江戸川 (江戸川区)|江戸川]]3-44-8 |地図 = |祭神 = [[経津主神|経津主命]] |社格 = |創建 = [[永禄]]7年（[[1564年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''上今井香取神社'''（かみいまいかとりじんじゃ）は[[東京都]][[江戸川区]]の[[神社]]。
== 概要 == [[1564年]]（[[永禄]]7年）に創建された。上今井村の鎮守であった。現在も本地仏として[[十一面観音]]を祀っており、[[神仏習合]]の名残が見られる<ref name="史跡散歩">内田定夫 著『江戸川区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド23)』[[学生社]]、1992年、76-77p</ref>。
[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）、鉄筋造りの社殿が新築された<ref name="史跡散歩"></ref>。
社殿の後ろに、[[富士塚]]がある。[[1930年]]（昭和5年）に[[富士講]]「今井割菱八行講」によって築造されたもので、江戸川区の有形民俗文化財に登録されている<ref name="史跡散歩"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[一之江駅]]より徒歩15分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *内田定夫 著『江戸川区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド23)』[[学生社]]、1992年
== 関連項目 == * [[富士塚]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Kamiimai Katori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kamiimai Katori-jinja (Edogawa, Edogawa) 01.jpg|240px]] | location = [[Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Edogawa Ward]], Edogawa 3-44-8 | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 41 | lat_sec = 0.2 | N/S = N | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 29.8 | E/W = E | map = | deity = {{ill|Keitsushinomi|ja|経津主命}} | type = | founded = [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564) | main_hall = | alternate_name = | marker = | festival = | rituals = }}
'''Kamiimai Katori Shrine''' (上今井香取神社, Kamiimai Katori Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokyo Metropolis]]'s [[Edogawa Ward]].
== History == It was founded in 1564 (Eiroku 7) as recorded in the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō (Kamiimai-mura)''<ref name="史跡散歩">Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992, pp. 76–77</ref>. The shrine once served as the guardian (chinju) of Kamiimai Village as noted in the same source. Even today, it continues to enshrine the honji-butsu in the form of [[Jūmen Kannon]], reflecting remnants of the syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism.
In 1974 (Shōwa 49), a new reinforced concrete shrine building was constructed<ref name="史跡散歩"/>.
Behind the shrine stands a Fujizuka (a tumulus dedicated to Fuji worship) that was built in 1930 (Shōwa 5) by the "Kamiimai Waribishi Hachigyō-kō" of the Fujikō, and is registered as a tangible folk cultural property by Edogawa Ward<ref name="史跡散歩"/>{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Cultural Properties == * The Fujizuka of Imai – Registered as a tangible folk cultural property and folk material by Edogawa Ward{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Access == * Approximately a 12‐minute walk from [[Ichinoe Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=35.68606,139.88345|goal=35.68329,139.89175|name=Route Guide}}).
== Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992. * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=【新編武蔵風土記稿】|volume=巻ノ29 葛飾郡ノ10|chapter=【上今井村】 香取社|id={{NDLJP|763979/66}}|publisher=Internal Ministry Geographical Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 上今井村}}}} * {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/040.html|title=Fujizuka of Imai|website=Edogawa Ward|accessdate=2020-05-16|date=2019-01-31|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区}}|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211114091000/https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/040.html |archive-date=2021-11-14|url-status=dead|url-status-date=2024-09-19}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * [[Fujizuka]] * [[Katori Shrine]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Kamiimai Katori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kamiimai Katori-jinja (Edogawa, Edogawa) 01.jpg|240px]] | location = [[Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Edogawa Ward]], Edogawa 3-44-8 | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 41 | lat_sec = 0.2 | N/S = N | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 29.8 | E/W = E | map = | deity = {{ill|Keitsushinomi|ja|経津主命|1=Futsunushi}} | type = | founded = [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564) | main_hall = | alternate_name = | marker = | festival = | rituals = }}
'''Kamiimai Katori Shrine''' (上今井香取神社, Kamiimai Katori Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokyo Metropolis]]'s [[Edogawa Ward]].
== History == It was founded in 1564 (Eiroku 7) as recorded in the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō (Kamiimai-mura)''<ref name="史跡散歩">Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992, pp. 76–77</ref>. The shrine once served as the guardian (chinju) of Kamiimai Village as noted in the same source. Even today, it continues to enshrine the honji-butsu in the form of [[Jūmen Kannon]], reflecting remnants of the syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism.
In 1974 (Shōwa 49), a new reinforced concrete shrine building was constructed<ref name="史跡散歩"/>.
Behind the shrine stands a Fujizuka (a tumulus dedicated to Fuji worship) that was built in 1930 (Shōwa 5) by the "Kamiimai Waribishi Hachigyō-kō" of the Fujikō, and is registered as a tangible folk cultural property by Edogawa Ward<ref name="史跡散歩"/>{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Cultural Properties == * The Fujizuka of Imai – Registered as a tangible folk cultural property and folk material by Edogawa Ward{{sfn|江戸川区}}.

The shrine once served as the guardian (chinju) of Kamiimai Village as noted in the same source. Even today, it continues to enshrine the honji-butsu in the form of [[Jūmen Kannon]], reflecting remnants of the syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism.
In 1974 (Shōwa 49), a new reinforced concrete shrine building was constructed<ref name="史跡散歩"/>.
Behind the shrine stands a Fujizuka (a tumulus dedicated to Fuji worship) that was built in 1930 (Shōwa 5) by the "Kamiimai Waribishi Hachigyō-kō" of the Fujikō, and is registered as a tangible folk cultural property by Edogawa Ward<ref name="史跡散歩"/>{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Cultural Properties == * The Fujizuka of Imai – Registered as a tangible folk cultural property and folk material by Edogawa Ward{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Access == * Approximately a 12‐minute walk from [[Ichinoe Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=35.68606,139.88345|goal=35.68329,139.89175|name=Route Guide}}).

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Kamiimai Katori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kamiimai Katori-jinja (Edogawa, Edogawa) 01.jpg|240px]] | location = [[Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Edogawa Ward]], Edogawa 3-44-8 | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 41 | lat_sec = 0.2 | N/S = N | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 29.8 | E/W = E | map = | deity = {{ill|Keitsushinomi|ja|経津主命|1=Futsunushi|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = | founded = [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564) | main_hall = | alternate_name = | marker = | festival = | rituals = }}
'''Kamiimai Katori Shrine''' (上今井香取神社, Kamiimai Katori Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokyo Metropolis]]'s [[Edogawa Ward]].
== History == It was founded in 1564 (Eiroku 7) as recorded in the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō (Kamiimai-mura)''<ref name="史跡散歩">Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992, pp. 76–77</ref>. The shrine once served as the guardian (chinju) of Kamiimai Village as noted in the same source. Even today, it continues to enshrine the honji-butsu in the form of [[Jūmen Kannon]], reflecting remnants of the syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism.
In 1974 (Shōwa 49), a new reinforced concrete shrine building was constructed<ref name="史跡散歩"/>.
Behind the shrine stands a Fujizuka (a tumulus dedicated to Fuji worship) that was built in 1930 (Shōwa 5) by the "Kamiimai Waribishi Hachigyō-kō" of the Fujikō, and is registered as a tangible folk cultural property by Edogawa Ward<ref name="史跡散歩"/>{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Cultural Properties == * The Fujizuka of Imai – Registered as a tangible folk cultural property and folk material by Edogawa Ward{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Access == * Approximately a 12‐minute walk from [[Ichinoe Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=35.68606,139.88345|goal=35.68329,139.89175|name=Route Guide}}).
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992. * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=【新編武蔵風土記稿】|volume=巻ノ29 葛飾郡ノ10|chapter=【上今井村】 香取社|id={{NDLJP|763979/66}}|publisher=Internal Ministry Geographical Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 上今井村}}}} * {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/040.html|title=Fujizuka of Imai|website=Edogawa Ward|accessdate=2020-05-16|date=2019-01-31|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区}}|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211114091000/https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/040.html |archive-date=2021-11-14|url-status=dead|url-status-date=2024-09-19}}

'''Kamiimai Katori Shrine''' (上今井香取神社, Kamiimai Katori Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo Metropolis]]'s [[Edogawa Ward]]. 

== History ==
It was founded in 1564 (Eiroku 7) as recorded in the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō (Kamiimai-mura)''<ref name="史跡散歩">Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992, pp. 76–77</ref>. The shrine once served as the guardian (chinju) of Kamiimai Village as noted in the same source. Even today, it continues to enshrine the honji-butsu in the form of [[Jūmen Kannon]], reflecting remnants of the syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism.

In 1974 (Shōwa 49), a new reinforced concrete shrine building was constructed<ref name="史跡散歩"/>.

Behind the shrine stands a Fujizuka (a tumulus dedicated to Fuji worship) that was built in 1930 (Shōwa 5) by the "Kamiimai Waribishi Hachigyō-kō" of the Fujikō, and is registered as a tangible folk cultural property by Edogawa Ward<ref name="史跡散歩"/>{{sfn|江戸川区}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* The Fujizuka of Imai – Registered as a tangible folk cultural property and folk material by Edogawa Ward{{sfn|江戸川区}}.

== Access ==
* Approximately a 12‐minute walk from [[:en:Ichinoe Station|Ichinoe Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=35.68606,139.88345|goal=35.68329,139.89175|name=Route Guide}}).

== Footnotes ==

{{Reflist}}

== References ==

* {{cite book|lang=ja|title=【新編武蔵風土記稿】|volume=巻ノ29 葛飾郡ノ10|chapter=【上今井村】 香取社|id={{NDLJP|763979/66}}|publisher=Internal Ministry Geographical Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 上今井村}}}}
* {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/040.html|title=Fujizuka of Imai|website=Edogawa Ward|accessdate=2020-05-16|date=2019-01-31|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区}}|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211114091000/https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/040.html |archive-date=2021-11-14|url-status=dead|url-status-date=2024-09-19}}

'''Kamiimai Katori Shrine''' (上今井香取神社, Kamiimai Katori Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo Metropolis]]'s [[Edogawa Ward]]. 

== History ==
It was founded in 1564 (Eiroku 7) as recorded in the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō (Kamiimai-mura)''<ref name="史跡散歩">Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992, pp. 76–77</ref>. The shrine once served as the guardian (chinju) of Kamiimai Village as noted in the same source. Even today, it continues to enshrine the honji-butsu in the form of [[Jūmen Kannon]], reflecting remnants of the syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism.

In 1974 (Shōwa 49), a new reinforced concrete shrine building was constructed<ref name="史跡散歩"/>.

Behind the shrine stands a Fujizuka (a tumulus dedicated to Fuji worship) that was built in 1930 (Shōwa 5) by the "Kamiimai Waribishi Hachigyō-kō" of the Fujikō, and is registered as a tangible folk cultural property by Edogawa Ward<ref name="史跡散歩"/>{{sfn|江戸川区}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* The Fujizuka of Imai – Registered as a tangible folk cultural property and folk material by Edogawa Ward{{sfn|江戸川区}}.

== Access ==
* Approximately a 12‐minute walk from [[:en:Ichinoe Station|Ichinoe Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=35.68606,139.88345|goal=35.68329,139.89175|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Kamiimai Katori Shrine''' (上今井香取神社, Kamiimai Katori Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo Metropolis]]'s [[Edogawa Ward]]. 

== History ==
It was founded in 1564 (Eiroku 7) as recorded in the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō (Kamiimai-mura)''<ref name="史跡散歩">Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992, pp. 76–77</ref>. The shrine once served as the guardian (chinju) of Kamiimai Village as noted in the same source. Even today, it continues to enshrine the honji-butsu in the form of [[Jūmen Kannon]], reflecting remnants of the syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism.

In 1974 (Shōwa 49), a new reinforced concrete shrine building was constructed<ref name="史跡散歩"/>.

Behind the shrine stands a Fujizuka (a tumulus dedicated to Fuji worship) that was built in 1930 (Shōwa 5) by the "Kamiimai Waribishi Hachigyō-kō" of the Fujikō, and is registered as a tangible folk cultural property by Edogawa Ward<ref name="史跡散歩"/>{{sfn|江戸川区}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* The Fujizuka of Imai – Registered as a tangible folk cultural property and folk material by Edogawa Ward{{sfn|江戸川区}}.

== Access ==
* Approximately a 12‐minute walk from [[:en:Ichinoe Station|Ichinoe Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=35.68606,139.88345|goal=35.68329,139.89175|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Kamiimai Katori Shrine''' (上今井香取神社, Kamiimai Katori Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo Metropolis]]'s [[Edogawa Ward]].
== History == It was founded in 1564 (Eiroku 7) as recorded in the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō (Kamiimai-mura)''<ref name="史跡散歩">Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992, pp. 76–77</ref>. The shrine once served as the guardian (chinju) of Kamiimai Village as noted in the same source. Even today, it continues to enshrine the honji-butsu in the form of [[Jūmen Kannon]], reflecting remnants of the syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism.
In 1974 (Shōwa 49), a new reinforced concrete shrine building was constructed<ref name="史跡散歩"/>.
Behind the shrine stands a Fujizuka (a tumulus dedicated to Fuji worship) that was built in 1930 (Shōwa 5) by the "Kamiimai Waribishi Hachigyō-kō" of the Fujikō, and is registered as a tangible folk cultural property by Edogawa Ward<ref name="史跡散歩"/>{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Cultural Properties == * The Fujizuka of Imai – Registered as a tangible folk cultural property and folk material by Edogawa Ward{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Access == * Approximately a 12‐minute walk from [[:en:Ichinoe Station|Ichinoe Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=35.68606,139.88345|goal=35.68329,139.89175|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Kamiimai Katori Shrine''' (上今井香取神社, Kamiimai Katori Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo Metropolis]]'s [[Edogawa Ward]].
== History == It was founded in 1564 (Eiroku 7) as recorded in the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō (Kamiimai-mura)''<ref name="史跡散歩">Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992, pp. 76–77</ref>. The shrine once served as the guardian (chinju) of Kamiimai Village as noted in the same source. Even today, it continues to enshrine the honji-butsu in the form of [[Jūmen Kannon]], reflecting remnants of the syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism.
In 1974 (Shōwa 49), a new reinforced concrete shrine building was constructed<ref name="史跡散歩"/>.
Behind the shrine stands a Fujizuka (a tumulus dedicated to Fuji worship) that was built in 1930 (Shōwa 5) by the "Kamiimai Waribishi Hachigyō-kō" of the Fujikō, and is registered as a tangible folk cultural property by Edogawa Ward<ref name="史跡散歩"/>{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Cultural Properties == * The Fujizuka of Imai – Registered as a tangible folk cultural property and folk material by Edogawa Ward{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Access == * Approximately a 12‐minute walk from {{ill|Ichinoe Station|en|Ichinoe Station|WD=Q1044276|lt=Ichinoe Station}} ({{OSM経路図|start=35.68606,139.88345|goal=35.68329,139.89175|name=Route Guide}}).
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992. * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=【新編武蔵風土記稿】|volume=巻ノ29 葛飾郡ノ10|chapter=【上今井村】 香取社|id={{NDLJP|763979/66}}|publisher=Internal Ministry Geographical Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 上今井村}}}} * {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/040.html|title=Fujizuka of Imai|website=Edogawa Ward|accessdate=2020-05-16|date=2019-01-31|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区}}|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211114091000/https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/040.html |archive-date=2021-11-14|url-status=dead|url-status-date=2024-09-19}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Fujizuka|en|Fujizuka|WD=Q5507555|lt=Fujizuka}} * [[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| | Name = Kamiimai Katori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kamiimai Katori-jinja (Edogawa, Edogawa) 01.jpg | location = [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Edogawa Ward]], Edogawa 3-44-8 | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 41 | lat_sec = 0.2 | N/S = N | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 29.8 | E/W = E | map = | deity = {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} | type = | founded = [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564) | main_hall = | alternate_name = | marker = | festival = | rituals = | location = {{ill|Edogawa|WD=Q11551026}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.683389|N|139.891611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kamiimai Katori Shrine''' (上今井香取神社, Kamiimai Katori Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo Metropolis]]'s [[Edogawa Ward]].
== History == It was founded in 1564 (Eiroku 7) as recorded in the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō (Kamiimai-mura)''<ref name="史跡散歩">Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992, pp. 76–77</ref>. The shrine once served as the guardian (chinju) of Kamiimai Village as noted in the same source. Even today, it continues to enshrine the honji-butsu in the form of [[Jūmen Kannon]], reflecting remnants of the syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism.
In 1974 (Shōwa 49), a new reinforced concrete shrine building was constructed<ref name="史跡散歩"/>.
Behind the shrine stands a Fujizuka (a tumulus dedicated to Fuji worship) that was built in 1930 (Shōwa 5) by the "Kamiimai Waribishi Hachigyō-kō" of the Fujikō, and is registered as a tangible folk cultural property by Edogawa Ward<ref name="史跡散歩"/>{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Cultural Properties == * The Fujizuka of Imai – Registered as a tangible folk cultural property and folk material by Edogawa Ward{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Access == * Approximately a 12‐minute walk from {{ill|Ichinoe Station|en|Ichinoe Station|WD=Q1044276|lt=Ichinoe Station}} ({{OSM経路図|start=35.68606,139.88345|goal=35.68329,139.89175|name=Route Guide}}).
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992. * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=【新編武蔵風土記稿】|volume=巻ノ29 葛飾郡ノ10|chapter=【上今井村】 香取社|id={{NDLJP|763979/66}}|publisher=Internal Ministry Geographical Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 上今井村}}}} * {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/040.html|title=Fujizuka of Imai|website=Edogawa Ward|accessdate=2020-05-16|date=2019-01-31|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区}}|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211114091000/https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/040.html |archive-date=2021-11-14|url-status=dead|url-status-date=2024-09-19}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Fujizuka|en|Fujizuka|WD=Q5507555|lt=Fujizuka}} * [[Katori Jingū|Katori Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| | Name = Kamiimai Katori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kamiimai Katori-jinja (Edogawa, Edogawa) 01.jpg | location = [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Edogawa Ward]], Edogawa 3-44-8 | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 41 | lat_sec = 0.2 | N/S = N | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 29.8 | E/W = E | map = | deity = {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} | type = | founded = [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564) | main_hall = | alternate_name = | marker = | festival = | rituals = | location = {{ill|Edogawa|WD=Q11551026|1=Edogawa}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.683389|N|139.891611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kamiimai Katori Shrine''' (上今井香取神社, Kamiimai Katori Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo Metropolis]]'s [[Edogawa Ward]].
== History == It was founded in 1564 (Eiroku 7) as recorded in the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō (Kamiimai-mura)''<ref name="史跡散歩">Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992, pp. 76–77</ref>. The shrine once served as the guardian (chinju) of Kamiimai Village as noted in the same source. Even today, it continues to enshrine the honji-butsu in the form of [[Jūmen Kannon]], reflecting remnants of the syncretism between Shinto and Buddhism.
In 1974 (Shōwa 49), a new reinforced concrete shrine building was constructed<ref name="史跡散歩"/>.
Behind the shrine stands a Fujizuka (a tumulus dedicated to Fuji worship) that was built in 1930 (Shōwa 5) by the "Kamiimai Waribishi Hachigyō-kō" of the Fujikō, and is registered as a tangible folk cultural property by Edogawa Ward<ref name="史跡散歩"/>{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Cultural Properties == * The Fujizuka of Imai – Registered as a tangible folk cultural property and folk material by Edogawa Ward{{sfn|江戸川区}}.
== Access == * Approximately a 12‐minute walk from {{ill|Ichinoe Station|en|Ichinoe Station|WD=Q1044276|lt=Ichinoe Station|1=Ichinoe Station}} ({{OSM経路図|start=35.68606,139.88345|goal=35.68329,139.89175|name=Route Guide}}).
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * Uchida Sadawo, ''Edogawa Ward Historical Site Walk (Tokyo Historical Sites Guide 23)'', [[Gakuseisha]], 1992. * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=【新編武蔵風土記稿】|volume=巻ノ29 葛飾郡ノ10|chapter=【上今井村】 香取社|id={{NDLJP|763979/66}}|publisher=Internal Ministry Geographical Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 上今井村}}}} * {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/040.html|title=Fujizuka of Imai|website=Edogawa Ward|accessdate=2020-05-16|date=2019-01-31|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区}}|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211114091000/https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/040.html |archive-date=2021-11-14|url-status=dead|url-status-date=2024-09-19}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Fujizuka|en|Fujizuka|WD=Q5507555|lt=Fujizuka|1=Fujizuka}} * [[Katori Jingū|Katori Shrine]]
